content: Welcome to the Morning Reflections Community! This is a space for WGSP 1490 AM listeners to connect, share encouragement, and carry Saturday mornings into the rest of the week. Glad you're here.
This Saturday (Jan 4, 7-9 AM on WGSP 1490 AM): we kick off the new year talking about what it means to carry hope when the tank is low. Special guest, Rev. Angela Dawson, joins us in-studio. Tune in.
New episode replay now available on the WGSP website: 'Grief Is Not the End' from our Feb 22 broadcast. Link in the episode-feedback channel. Thank you to everyone who called in.
Mark your calendars: our 7-year anniversary episode is coming up in March! We've prepared something special. 7-9 AM on 1490 AM. Share the dial.
Listened to Saturday's episode on the Greenline walk this morning. That line - 'Rest is not retreat, it's preparation' - I needed that. Thank you, Rebecca.
I've been listening to Morning Reflections since 2021. Finally found the Discord! Memphis faithful represent.
Welcome, Carolyn! So glad you found us. Three years of Saturday mornings - that means you were there through some of my rougher seasons too. Grateful for that.
Does anyone have the book Rebecca mentioned last month - the Henri Nouwen one about the wounded healer? Trying to track it down.
Darlene - it's 'The Wounded Healer' by Henri Nouwen. Midtown Pages usually keeps it in stock. Tell them I sent you.
The Feb 8 episode on forgiveness hit different. I've listened to it three times. That story about the fig tree - just right.
I shared the Mar 15 segment on compassion fatigue with my chaplaincy team at Golden Heart. It opened up a real conversation. Good work, Rebecca.
Angela, that means a lot. I was nervous about that one - you don't always know if the thing you needed to say is the thing someone else needed to hear. Grateful it landed.
Shoutout to Covenant Insurance for sponsoring the show. They've been there since the early days.
Please keep my family in prayer this week - my father-in-law is in the hospital. Grateful for this community.
Roy, lifting your family up. Hospitals can feel like the edge of everything - know that presence matters, yours included. We're holding you in prayer.

Books mentioned on air this season so far: 'The Wounded Healer' - Nouwen; 'The Book of Awakening' - Mark Nepo; 'Braving the Wilderness' - Brené Brown; 'Jesus and the Disinherited' - Howard Thurman. All worth your time.
I'd add 'Prodigal Summer' by Barbara Kingsolver if anyone wants something that's not strictly theology but still feels like a spiritual experience. Gorgeous writing.
Good morning, Grace family. Reminder that this Sunday we celebrate our homecoming service. Please arrive by 7:45 AM to help with setup. Blessings.

Community Health Fair date confirmed: Saturday, October 3, 2026, church grounds 10 AM - 2 PM. Need volunteers for check-in, blood pressure screenings, and food tables. Sign up in the health-fair-planning channel.

Deacon Mosley and I have confirmed the health fair lineup: Bluff City Community College nursing students will staff BP/glucose screenings. I'll coordinate the devotional opening at 10 AM. Mitchell's Flowers donating centerpieces. More details to come.
I can work the check-in table for the full four hours. Also happy to help with setup at 9 AM.
I'll handle the food table and coordinate with the kitchen team. Same as last year.
Sunday January 18 volunteer roster: Ushers - Earl, Linda. Greeters - Tamara, Jerome. Nursery - open (please reach out if available). Rebecca is delivering the opening meditation. Service at 8 AM.
I can cover nursery on January 18 if we still need someone. Just let me know by Thursday.
Linda, thank you - I'll let Deacon Mosley know you're available. And yes, I'll be there for the opening meditation. See everyone Sunday.
Sat with a family in the ICU at 3 AM, made the air a little lighter.
Morning Reflections live from the Beale Street studio this week.
Friday groceries and dinner at Mom's in South Memphis.
Greenline walk with worship music, two to three miles.
Sunday dinner ministry — pot roast, mac and cheese, peach cobbler.
Morning pages before dawn, coffee steam in the dark kitchen.
Booked next week's radio guest — a Memphis musician for the devotional.
Palliative-care debrief with the team, then the ethics committee.
Brenda's voice on the phone every evening, the anchor.
Garden in the fading light — zinnias, lavender, the bird feeders.
CPE hours at 28 of 50, mentoring the two residents.
Naomi checked in twice this week, my old soul.
Chamomile on the porch swing with Mercy at dusk.
Withings weigh-in, down twelve; Dexcom steady, A1C at 6.4.
Playlist curated for the show — Lauren Daigle and the Gettys.
Health-fair planning with Deacon Mosley at Grace Community.
Therapy with Dr. Whitfield — compassion fatigue and boundaries.
Marcus called about the broadcasting internship, so proud.
Saturday yoga video before the live broadcast.
Tithe to Grace Community before I budget anything else.
Cafe Eclectic brunch with Sandra, our tradition.
Sponsor check-in with Covenant Insurance and Mitchell's Flowers.
Oura readiness low today — honored it and slowed down.
Interfaith event with Rev. Dawson, the clergy network.
